questions.q: What does a rotary encoder measure in a robot joint?
questions.explanation: Rotary encoders detect the angular position of a shaft by counting pulses or reading absolute position codes. Velocity is derived by differentiating position over time. Temperature, current, and vibration require different sensors (thermistors, current sensors, accelerometers).

questions.q: Why is an IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit) useful in mobile robotics?
options: It measures the robot's exact GPS coordinates
It detects acceleration, rotation, and orientation without external references
It determines how much battery power is remaining
It controls the speed of the wheels
questions.explanation: An IMU contains accelerometers and gyroscopes to measure linear acceleration and angular velocity. It enables dead-reckoning and attitude estimation without GPS. GPS coordinates are from a different sensor; battery and motor control are separate functions.

questions.q: A lidar (light detection and ranging) sensor is primarily used for:
options: Creating 2D/3D distance maps and detecting obstacles
Capturing color images of the environment
questions.explanation: Lidar uses laser pulses to measure distances to objects in the environment, creating point clouds or 2D scans. It's essential for obstacle detection and SLAM. Color imaging requires cameras; thermal imaging uses infrared; torque needs load cells or strain gauges.

questions.q: What is the key difference between a servo motor and a standard DC motor?
options: Servo motors are smaller and cheaper
Servo motors have built-in feedback control for precise position and speed
Servo motors can only rotate in one direction
questions.explanation: Servo motors integrate an encoder and controller, enabling closed-loop control of position to a target setpoint. Standard DC motors lack feedback and require external control to maintain position. Cost, size, and direction are independent features unrelated to servo definition.

questions.q: How does a stepper motor differ from a servo motor in terms of control?
options: Stepper motors move in discrete angular steps without position feedback; servo motors use feedback for continuous control
questions.explanation: Stepper motors move in fixed increments (steps) per pulse, assuming no missed steps, without built-in feedback. Servo motors close a feedback loop to verify position and adjust continuously. Both have applications; the choice depends on accuracy requirements and power budget.

questions.q: What does a potentiometer measure in a robot joint?
options: Joint position as a voltage that changes with shaft angle
questions.explanation: A potentiometer is a variable resistor whose resistance changes with mechanical rotation. Connected to a voltage divider, it produces a voltage proportional to position, enabling position feedback. Current, efficiency, and light are measured by different sensors.

questions.q: An incremental encoder outputs 2048 pulses per revolution. What angular resolution does this provide?
questions.explanation: Angular resolution = 360° / 2048 = 0.176°. Incremental encoders count pulses from a reference, giving discrete position updates. This differs from absolute encoders which directly report current position.

questions.q: Why is a gripper (end-effector) often equipped with force/torque sensors?
options: To measure the weight of grasped objects only
To provide feedback for compliant grasping without crushing or dropping items
To prevent the gripper from overheating
To calibrate the robot's forward kinematics
questions.explanation: Force/torque sensors enable closed-loop force control during grasping. Without feedback, the gripper cannot adjust grip strength—it may crush fragile items or drop heavy ones. Temperature, weight estimation, and kinematics require different approaches.

questions.q: What is the function of a brush in a brushed DC motor?
options: It cleans dust from the motor
It switches electrical current to the commutator coils to maintain rotation
questions.explanation: Brushes maintain electrical contact with the rotating commutator, reversing current direction in coils to sustain continuous rotation. This is fundamental to brushed DC motor operation. Dust removal is incidental; other options describe unrelated functions.

questions.q: A camera on a robot arm is positioned to observe the workspace. What type of sensor is this for the robot's perception?
questions.explanation: Exteroceptive sensors (camera, lidar) sense the external environment. Proprioceptive sensors (encoders, IMU) sense the robot's own state. Kinesthetic sensing is human-specific. Cameras are purely sensors for perception, not actuators.
